message:
test(fcp-core): expand EventSeverity + MeshPlacementHint test pins

Why
---
Two enum-shaped types in fcp-core had partial test pins: ordering
was locked but the Display-vs-serde-tag agreement and the negative
behaviour (rejection of non-canonical wire forms) were not. This
commit closes both gaps so the externally-visible contract for
both types is fully pinned.

The Display impl for EventSeverity (added in commit f652c340f
under flywheel_connectors-XXXX) made it possible to assert the
three-way Display = `as_str` = serde tag agreement. The mesh
placement hint pin uses the existing `as_str` to lock the same
agreement, plus a BTreeSet-based ordering test that catches
accidental drift in the declared preference order without
duplicating the existing windowed comparison test.

What
----
- crates/fcp-core/tests/event_severity_ordering.rs:
  - Pull the ASCENDING array up into a const so all tests share it.
  - Add CASES const mapping each variant to its lowercase tag.
  - New `event_severity_display_matches_json_tag`: for each
    variant, asserts Display, serde_json::to_string, and
    serde_json round-trip all agree.
  - New `event_severity_cbor_tag_is_text_and_roundtrips`: pins
    that the CBOR encoding is the text major-type of the canonical
    lowercase tag, and that round-trip recovers the variant.
  - New `event_severity_rejects_noncanonical_json_tags`: pins
    rejection of "Info" (PascalCase), "WARNING" (uppercase),
    "warn" (truncated), "critical_alert" (made-up), and ""
    (empty).

- crates/fcp-core/tests/mesh_placement_hint_serde_ordering.rs:
  - Module-level comment explaining why this is the affinity
    surface for fcp-core (zone-aware mesh placement preferences,
    stable Ord + Display semantics for policy code).
  - New `display_matches_stable_affinity_tokens`: asserts that
    `as_str` and `to_string` both produce the canonical wire token
    for every variant.
  - New `sorted_affinity_set_uses_declared_preference_order`:
    inserts variants into a BTreeSet in scrambled order and
    asserts the iteration order matches the declared ASCENDING
    array — catches accidental Ord derive drift that the existing
    windowed-comparison test would miss.

Compatibility
-------------
Pure test additions; assertion-only, no production code change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
